claim: For all integers n >= 1, sigma(n) * phi(n) is at most n^2.
predicate: all(sigma(n) * phi(n) <= n ** 2 for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all n >= 1, sigma(n) * phi(n) is divisible by n.
predicate: all((sigma(n) * phi(n)) % n == 0 for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all n >= 1, if n is prime, then sigma(n) * phi(n) = n^2 - n.
predicate: all((not is_prime(n)) or (sigma(n) * phi(n) == n ** 2 - n) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all n >= 1, if n is square-free, then sigma(n) * phi(n) <= n^2 - n.
predicate: all((any(vp(n, p) > 1 for p in range(2, n + 1) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0)) or (sigma(n) * phi(n) <= n ** 2 - n) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all n >= 1, if n is the product of two distinct primes, then sigma(n) * phi(n) = (n^2 - n) - (p + q) + 1 where p and q are the prime factors of n.
predicate: all((tau(n) != 2 or not is_prime(n)) or (sigma(n) * phi(n) == (n ** 2 - n) - (min([p for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0]) + max([p for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0])) + 1) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all n >= 1, if n is square-free and even, then sigma(n) * phi(n) is divisible by 2n.
predicate: all((n % 2 != 0 or any(vp(n, p) > 1 for p in range(2, n + 1) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0)) or ((sigma(n) * phi(n)) % (2 * n) == 0) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all n >= 1, if n is a perfect square, then sigma(n) * phi(n) is divisible by n^(3/2).
predicate: all((round(n ** 0.5) ** 2 != n) or ((sigma(n) * phi(n)) % (round(n ** 1.5)) == 0) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all n >= 1, if n is a power of a prime p, then sigma(n) * phi(n) = n^2 * (1 - 1/p).
predicate: all((len([p for p in range(2, n + 1) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0]) != 1) or (sigma(n) * phi(n) == n ** 2 * (1 - 1 / min([p for p in range(2, n + 1) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0]))) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all n >= 1, if n is square-free and divisible by 6, then sigma(n) * phi(n) is divisible by 12n.
predicate: all((n % 6 != 0 or any(vp(n, p) > 1 for p in range(2, n + 1) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0)) or ((sigma(n) * phi(n)) % (12 * n) == 0) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all n >= 1, if n is a perfect square, then sigma(n) * phi(n) <= n^2 - n^(3/2).
predicate: all((round(n ** 0.5) ** 2 != n) or (sigma(n) * phi(n) <= n ** 2 - round(n ** 1.5)) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all n >= 1, if n is square-free or a prime power, then sigma(n) * phi(n) <= n^2 - n.
predicate: all((not (any(vp(n, p) > 1 for p in range(2, n + 1) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0) or len([p for p in range(2, n + 1) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0]) == 1)) or (sigma(n) * phi(n) <= n ** 2 - n) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all n >= 1, if n is square-free and divisible by 30, then sigma(n) * phi(n) is divisible by 120n.
predicate: all((n % 30 != 0 or any(vp(n, p) > 1 for p in range(2, n + 1) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0)) or ((sigma(n) * phi(n)) % (120 * n) == 0) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all n >= 1, if n is the product of two distinct primes or a prime power, then sigma(n) * phi(n) <= n^2 - n.
predicate: all((not (tau(n) == 2 and is_prime(min([p for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0])) and is_prime(max([p for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0]))) and not (len([p for p in range(2, n + 1) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0]) == 1)) or (sigma(n) * phi(n) <= n ** 2 - n) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all n >= 1, if n is a perfect square, then sigma(n) * phi(n) is divisible by n^(3/2) and <= n^2 - n^(3/2).
predicate: all((round(n ** 0.5) ** 2 != n) or ((sigma(n) * phi(n)) % (round(n ** 1.5)) == 0 and sigma(n) * phi(n) <= n ** 2 - round(n ** 1.5)) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all n >= 1, if n is square-free and divisible by 6, then sigma(n) * phi(n) is divisible by 12n, and if divisible by 30, by 120n.
predicate: all((n % 6 != 0 or any(vp(n, p) > 1 for p in range(2, n + 1) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0)) or ((sigma(n) * phi(n)) % (12 * n) == 0 and (n % 30 != 0 or (sigma(n) * phi(n)) % (120 * n) == 0)) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all n >= 1, if n is a prime power or the product of two distinct primes, then sigma(n) * phi(n) <= n^2 - n.
predicate: all((not (len([p for p in range(2, n + 1) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0]) == 1 or (tau(n) == 2 and is_prime(min([p for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0])) and is_prime(max([p for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0])))) or (sigma(n) * phi(n) <= n ** 2 - n) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all n >= 1, if n is a perfect square, then sigma(n) * phi(n) <= n^2 - sqrt(n)^3 and is divisible by sqrt(n)^3.
predicate: all((round(n ** 0.5) ** 2 != n) or (sigma(n) * phi(n) <= n ** 2 - round(n ** 1.5) and (sigma(n) * phi(n)) % round(n ** 1.5) == 0) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all n >= 1, if n is square-free, then sigma(n) * phi(n) <= n^2 - n, and if divisible by 6, divisible by 12n.
